repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry selects a Hölder version of an exact initiation-time illness-death risk across compatible full-data extensions.

published_prior_art.cite: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased studies the same exact-time nonregularity and smoothed illness-death intervention setting, though not this compatible-extension Hölder selection.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest Stat entries concern dose-response minimax localization or weak-event ratios, not an exogenous planned illness-death initiation-hazard EIF.

published_prior_art.cite: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased derives an EIF for its natural-hazard-derived normalized-window intervention; the fixed-q_b intervention is distinct but adjacent.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry supplies this product-integral application.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI supplies the underlying Hölder bias geometry; the remaining intervention identity is a routine application.
standard uncensored g-computation/martingale-gradient reduction; it is a diagnostic check rather than a new published-result comparison.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference — bounded-count PN/PS-ratio inference, not a CAR illness-death marked-Poisson score experiment.

published_prior_art.cite: SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous gives static continuous-exposure effective-size theory; AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment and ColangeloLee2023ContinuousDML give Gaussian continuous-treatment inference, not a finite-count multistate point-process limit.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference — a close bounded-count honest-diameter question for PN/PS ratios, but with a different estimand and no initiation-time intervention.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI supplies Hölder bias-aware interval geometry, but not learned CAR illness-death scores or bounded-effective-count calibration.

soundness_flags.label: ass:car
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed equality conditions both censoring intensities on the same observed history and is automatic once P_full has observed margin P; it is not the standard CAR/noninformative-censoring restriction required by its name.

soundness_flags.label: def:eif-components and lem:uncensored-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: W_{1,P,b} is declared wherever q_b(u)>0 although a_b(u) is defined only where Qbar_b(u)>0; allowed kernels can have q_b(u_star+b)>0 while Qbar_b(u_star+b)=0, leaving the formula undefined.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:critical-phase
soundness_flags.one_line: Its declared dependency def:critical-submodel fixes b_n=lambda/n, so the requested nb_n→infinity Gaussian regime has no defined triangular submodel, statistic family, or assumptions separate from the critical regime.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:observed-hazard-validity and ass:compatible-extension
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The causal reading assumes that compatible full-data extensions obey the stated intervention-hazard validity restriction; this is not recovered from the observed law.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Extension-invariance and theta(P) as an exact-time causal risk are conditional on P_full belonging to mathfrak E(P) with observed-hazard validity; the phase-transition questions are conditional observed-law statistical claims.

source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 3–6, §§2.2–3.1; especially p. 6 paragraph beginning “In reality there will be censoring”
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper distinguishes full- and observed-filtration interventions, defines its own natural-hazard-derived normalized window, derives its EIF, and merely assumes censoring is CAR as defined in Tsiatis; it does not formulate CAR as the core's equality of two intensities both conditioned on observed history.

The paper studies exponentially tilted stochastic interventions for static continuous exposures, deriving EIF, effective-sample-size n/delta minimax lower bounds, convergence rates, and DML bias bounds.
The paper proposes minimum-variance weighting and local-linear GDRF estimation, with uniform Bahadur representation, bootstrap bands, undersmoothing selection, and bias-control bands.

source_verification_receipts.version: Quantitative Economics 11(1), 2020, pp. 1–39, version of record
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article develops honest bias-aware kernel/local-polynomial confidence intervals under Hölder restrictions, including efficiency and bandwidth comparisons.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of Business & Economic Statistics 44(1), 2026, pp. 67–79
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper gives kernel-localized, cross-fitted doubly robust DML inference for average continuous-treatment response and partial-effect targets under nuisance-rate conditions.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 119(546), 2024, pp. 1541–1553
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The cited JASA article develops a cross-fitted doubly robust estimator for treatment-specific survival curves with pointwise and uniform asymptotic linearity under nuisance conditions.
The article derives minimax CATE rates in a Hölder-smooth nonparametric model and gives a local-polynomial estimator under high-level optimality conditions.

source_verification_receipts.version: JRSS B 79(4), 2017, pp. 1229–1245, version of record
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article develops kernel-smoothed doubly robust continuous-treatment effect estimation, with a stochastic-intervention EIF, asymptotic theory, and bandwidth selection.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace ass:car with a genuine full-filtration CAR/noninformative-censoring condition, then state the observed-data reduction that supplies the censoring weights.

Define a separate Gaussian-regime triangular experiment with b_n downarrow 0 and nb_n→infinity, including its centered statistic and nuisance-rate inputs, rather than placing it under the critical submodel.
Sharpen the honest-diameter OEQ into a phase diagram: name a marked-Poisson-calibrated interval and a concrete critical-regime diameter order or modulus/lower-bound route.

recommended_next_step: Revise the CAR condition, active-support definition of W_1, and missing diverging-effective-count experiment; the repaired rare-initiation phase-transition kernel remains field-grade and can then advance to Stage 0.
